name: RethinkDB Real-Time Document Database
what_it_does: Open-source real-time document database with native query API — enabling developers to build real-time apps by pushing database change notifications to applications via changefeeds (live queries), with a JSON document model, a custom query language (ReQL), and horizontal sharding — historically significant as a pioneer of push-based real-time database architecture.
use_cases: Building real-time collaborative apps via RethinkDB changefeeds for live data synchronization
Powering multiplayer games via RethinkDB for real-time state synchronization
Real-time analytics dashboards via RethinkDB changefeeds for live metric updates
Building reactive microservices via RethinkDB for event-driven database triggers
Self-hosting a real-time document store via RethinkDB for privacy-preserving live apps
not_for: New production deployments requiring commercial support (RethinkDB company closed 2016)
High-write-throughput workloads (RethinkDB had write scaling limitations)
Teams needing active commercial support (use MongoDB Atlas, CouchDB, or Firestore instead)
best_when: A self-sufficient team needs a self-hosted real-time document database with push-based change notifications — accepting the risk that RethinkDB is community-maintained without commercial support since 2016.
avoid_when: You need commercial support, production SLAs, active development, or are building a new system (use Firebase Realtime DB, MongoDB Change Streams, or CouchDB instead).

auth.notes: Username/password auth (optional — not enabled by default). HTTP API (port 8080) for admin access — no auth required by default. Driver connections on port 28015 — username/password optional. TLS for driver connections (configurable). No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Admin web UI on port 8080 — restrict access. Self-hosted only.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; company closed 2016; community maintained
pricing.notes: IMPORTANT: RethinkDB company went out of business in October 2016. Code donated to Linux Foundation (Cloud Native Computing Foundation). Apache 2.0 license after handover. Community maintained since 2016. Originally started 2009, raised $12.2M. Main innovation: changefeeds (live queries) — ahead of its time. JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Java drivers maintained by community. Used by: Platzi, Horizon (Firebase alternative). Alternative: Compose.io (now IBM) offered hosted RethinkDB. Inspired: CockroachDB developers, Firebase Realtime Database concepts. Changefeed concept now in MongoDB Change Streams, CouchDB. Active community on Discord/GitHub despite company closure.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Driver-based API (ReQL) — not REST for data operations. HTTP API for admin only. No auth by default. Self-hosted. Apache 2.0. Company closed 2016 — community maintained. Changefeeds for real-time push. JavaScript/Python/Ruby/Java drivers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All read queries fully idempotent. Insert with conflict handling (error/replace/update). Upsert operations idempotent. Changefeed subscription state managed by driver.
known_agent_gotchas: COMPANY CLOSED 2016 — no commercial support; community-maintained Apache 2.0 project
Not a REST API — primary interface via language drivers (ReQL DSL), not HTTP
Admin HTTP API (port 8080) has no auth by default — restrict by firewall/network
Driver port 28015 — no auth required by default; enable auth for production
Changefeeds are the unique feature — standard query alternatives in MongoDB Change Streams
ReQL is a custom query language — not SQL or MongoDB query syntax

security_score_components.security_notes: No auth by default — HIGH RISK if exposed without firewall. TLS optional (must configure). Admin HTTP API unauthenticated by default. Company closed 2016 — security patches community-dependent. Apache 2.0 open source. User-controlled data residency. Should not be used for production without proper network isolation and auth configuration.
